lesson.menu_title: Telling the Full Story -- Past Continuous for the Unexpected
lesson.menu_desc: Tell the story of an unexpected moment: was / were + verb-ing for the background, past simple for what happened, with 'when' and 'while' -- 32 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 10 -- Telling the Full Story
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Maria Clara | Lesson 10 -- Telling the Full Story

listenings.text: Maria, before your trip, I want to tell you one thing. In every official dinner, something small goes wrong. The interpreter is late, a phone rings, the schedule changes out of the blue. It is normal. When it happens, do not panic. Take one moment, breathe, and speak with simple words. You do not need perfect English. You need calm English. Last year, while we were visiting a university in Lisbon, the microphone stopped working in the middle of my talk. I was not ready for it. But I stayed calm, I smiled, and I kept going. Nobody remembers the microphone. They remember that I finished. You are ready for this. You are not a beginner anymore. You are improving every single day.

I want to tell you about a moment last month. It was a normal Tuesday. I was preparing the agenda for the delegation, and my assistant was checking the flights. Everything was calm. Then, out of the blue, the phone rang. It was an urgent call: our meeting with the council was moving to the next morning. At that moment, everyone in the office reacted. People were talking, the printer was running, and the schedule was changing in front of us. But I did not panic. While my team was calling the hotel, I was writing the new plan. In one hour, the situation was under control. It was not an easy day, but we handled it together, and I am proud of that.

extra_audio.text: It was the first official dinner of the trip. Maria Clara was sitting next to the rector, and her interpreter was translating every sentence. Then, out of the blue, the interpreter received an urgent message and left the room. For a moment, Maria Clara was alone with the rector. She did not panic. While the waiters were serving the coffee, she stayed calm and she started to speak in simple English.

paras: It was the first official dinner of the trip. Ana, a Brazilian director, was sitting next to the rector. Her interpreter was translating every sentence, and Ana was listening carefully. Everything was calm.

Then, out of the blue, something unexpected happened. The interpreter received an urgent message and left the room. For a moment, Ana was alone with the rector, and everyone at the table was looking at her.

Ana did not panic. While the waiters were serving the coffee, she took a breath and she spoke. Her English was simple, but it was clear. The rector smiled. It was not perfect, but it was hers. In that moment, Ana understood something important: she was not a beginner anymore. She was improving.
reading.source: Institutional & Travel English -- narrative written for Maria Clara's package

reading.prompt: What is the main idea of this story?
choices: Ana failed because her English was not perfect at the dinner.
When something unexpected happens, you can stay calm and speak -- and that is how you see you are improving.
You should never go to an official dinner without an interpreter.

items: At the dinner, Ana was sitting next to the rector.
t
"Ana, a Brazilian director, was sitting next to the rector."
The interpreter stayed with Ana for the whole evening.
f
"The interpreter received an urgent message and left the room."
Ana panicked and said nothing to the rector.
f
"Ana did not panic... she took a breath and she spoke."
Ana's English that night was perfect.
f
"It was not perfect, but it was hers."
At the end, Ana felt she was improving.
t
"She was not a beginner anymore. She was improving."

bank.label: Telling the story: the background and the surprise
items: At 8 o'clock, we were waiting for the delegation.
I was presenting the report when the phone rang.
While we were having dinner, the rector made an announcement.
They were not expecting the news.
What were you doing when it happened?
It happened out of the blue, but I stayed calm.

items.situation: Tell me about a moment when something unexpected happened at work. Start with: "I was..."
tips: I was preparing the agenda when the news arrived.
Use was / were + verb-ing for the background.

items.situation: The interpreter left the room. What were you doing at that moment?
tips: I was talking to the rector when the interpreter left.
Background in past continuous, the surprise in past simple.

items.situation: Describe an official dinner. What were the people doing?
tips: The waiters were serving the coffee.
Everyone was listening to the speech.

items.situation: Something went wrong out of the blue. How did you stay calm?
tips: I took a moment, and I stayed calm.
Then I reacted with simple words.

items.situation: Finish the story: "While we were having the meeting, ..."
tips: One long action (were having) + one short action (went out).

items.situation: Tell me one situation you handled well. What happened?
tips: It was an urgent situation, but I handled it.
I am not a beginner anymore -- I am improving.

pressure.title: Answer live
items: What were you doing when you received the news about the China trip?
Tell me about a difficult moment last year. Do not stop for ten seconds.
Something unexpected is happening now in the meeting. React in English.

pressure.text: Background first (was / were + verb-ing), then the surprise (past simple). Calm English, not perfect English.
